name: bryn_wisp
description: Marsh-Child Bryn's show-a-Fairy-type flavor errand (BRAND-NEW cast member, doc 01 §2 #6 of 6; deliberately NO register slot, no combat, no economy). Both branches of sidequest/bryn/show_fairy in one run: first the NO-FAIRY path - the party is rebuilt to a single non-fairy Garchomp, verified fairy-free by the same partyhas probe the function uses (rcon guard, so a leaked fairy from a prior run fails LOUDLY instead of false-passing), and the click lands the squint actionbar with no latch and no lantern - then flabebe level=15 joins mid-scenario and the same button pays out: the Wisp-Lantern keepsake (a named soul lantern from npc_gift/wisp_lantern), the eyes-go-wide tellraw, and the bryn_wisp_done latch. (Needle choice: Bryn's kid-speech runs are quote-wrapped in the mcfunctions, which scenario_lint's "text" extractor cannot see into, so the waits target the unquoted narrator prefixes - 'Bryn squints at your party' / 'eyes go wide'; the second, fully quote-wrapped drift-line tellraw renders but is not needle-asserted.) The re-open lands on the lantern-friends done entry (prio 20) and its 'Where do the wisps go?' button delivers the Fairy Shrine breadcrumb (rumors/shrine_fairy, gated not defeated_fairy_shrine_leader - removed defensively in the preamble). Cleanup claws the flabebe back (party slot 2) so later fairy-probe assumptions stay deterministic. Bryn WANDERS (ambient_wander) - find_npc uses the default 48b radius from the boardwalk latch point.

steps.comment: -- preamble: tp FIRST (loaded chunks for the latch spawn). Site = the fen boardwalk latch at 1064.5 64 2470.5 - AUTHORED y=64 PLACEHOLDER, not yet ground-probed (see _probe_pending): confirm the real surface Y before the first live run. Near-canon reference: Sedge's live-verified tp at 1058 78 2478 suggests the causeway really sits near y64, but probe anyway --

-- (screen.close above = leftover-dialog guard) reset (idempotent re-runs): the errand latch, the probe scratch tag, the keepsake; defeated_fairy_shrine_leader removed defensively so the done-entry wisp button's no_ gate holds (mystic_mirror's defeated_mystic_leader precedent) --
-- party: empty all 6 slots (stadium_wave precedent), then ONE known non-fairy stand-in so the no-fairy branch is deterministic (no battle here - the level is irrelevant) --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative givemon garchomp level=50

steps.comment: -- settle (latch spawn + band re-derivation), then the fairy-free guard: probe with the SAME partyhas primitive show_fairy uses; if a fairy leaked into the party this fails loudly here instead of false-passing beat 1 --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative partyhas fairy bryn_fairy_ok

steps.comment: -- beat 1: the NO-FAIRY path. Default entry (chasing wisps), click the show button with a fairy-less party: squint actionbar, no latch, no lantern --

expect_screen_text: Shh — you'll scare it! …Aw. It went under the boardwalk. The blue ones only come out at dusk, and they never let me catch up.
Da says the wisps are just marsh-light. But marsh-light doesn't WAIT for you at the bend. Hey — trainers carry glimmery ones, don't they? The pink-sparkly kind? Show me one! Just once!

steps.comment: -- beat 2: the fairy joins (assignment-canon flabebe level=15), same button pays out: latch + Wisp-Lantern + both tellraw lines --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative givemon flabebe level=15

steps.comment: -- beat 3: the lantern-friends done entry (prio 20, positive bryn_wisp_done) + the Fairy Shrine breadcrumb button --

expect_screen_text: You still have the lantern? Good. Lysira says a lantern only counts if somebody is under it.
The wisps went shrine-way again last night. Past the last lantern, where the boardwalk stops being brave. If you go, tell them Bryn says hello.

steps.wait_chat: a shrine older than the town

steps.comment: -- cleanup: claw the flabebe back (slot 2 - the garchomp stand-in stays slot 1) so no later scenario inherits a surprise fairy; the lantern rides as a keepsake, preamble clears it --
